title: MetaML-Pro: Cross-Stage Design Flow Automation for Efficient Deep Learning Acceleration
preliminary_rationale: Explicitly mentions cross-stage design-flow automation and deep-learning acceleration; directly aligned with lifecycle/toolchain concerns.

title: hls4ml: A Flexible, Open-Source Platform for Deep Learning Acceleration on Reconfigurable Hardware
preliminary_rationale: Strong open-source workflow/platform angle linking ML models to FPGA-oriented hardware generation and deployment.

title: da4ml: Distributed arithmetic for real-time neural networks on FPGAs
preliminary_rationale: Likely relevant as a workflow-capable FPGA deployment/co-design framework tightly linked to hardware-aware ML realization.

title: Preplacement net length and timing estimation by customized graph neural network
preliminary_rationale: Directly addresses ML inside the physical-design flow with early prediction and integration into upstream design decisions.

title: VLSI placement parameter optimization using deep reinforcement learning
preliminary_rationale: Clear ML-for-EDA optimization paper on placement tuning; good candidate for flow-aware optimization discussion.

title: A survey of graph neural networks for electronic design automation
preliminary_rationale: Secondary study directly on GNNs for EDA; strong candidate for state-of-the-art framing and gap identification.

title: Generalizable cross-graph embedding for GNN-based congestion prediction
preliminary_rationale: EDA-specific, ML-specific, and centered on prediction within physical-design flow; likely includable.

title: On joint learning for solving placement and routing in chip design
preliminary_rationale: Strong EDA relevance and multi-stage physical-design flavor; good backward-snowballing candidate.

title: RL-Sizer: VLSI Gate Sizing for Timing Optimization using Deep Reinforcement Learning
preliminary_rationale: Direct ML-driven optimization for a concrete EDA stage, with likely strong discussion value for limitations and integration.

title: The Law of Attraction: Affinity-Aware Placement Optimization Using Graph Neural Networks
preliminary_rationale: Highly relevant graph-learning paper for placement optimization within EDA flow.

title: A customized graph neural network model for guiding analog IC placement
preliminary_rationale: Useful to broaden the review toward analog/layout automation while remaining EDA-centered.

title: Accurate operation delay prediction for FPGA HLS using graph neural networks
preliminary_rationale: Very close to your HLS/early-estimation cluster and likely includable under the same rationale as P093/P145.

title: AutoDNNchip: An automated dnn chip predictor and builder for both FPGAs and ASICs
preliminary_rationale: Strong HW/SW co-design and automation flavor, bridging ML models and hardware implementation choices.

title: Best of both worlds: Automl codesign of a cnn and its hardware accelerator
preliminary_rationale: Likely strong match for automated HW/SW co-design with ML-guided search across model/hardware choices.

title: Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network-Based Imitation Learning for Gate Sizing Acceleration
preliminary_rationale: EDA-specific optimization paper on gate sizing; good fit for ML-enhanced design-flow tasks.

title: LHNN: lattice hypergraph neural network for VLSI congestion prediction
preliminary_rationale: Another concrete ML-for-physical-design paper with strong EDA alignment.

title: Placement Optimization via PPA-Directed Graph Clustering
preliminary_rationale: Likely relevant to physical-design optimization, but title alone does not make ML explicit; inspect abstract/full text before inclusion.

title: ARCO: Adaptive Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning-Based Hardware/Software Co-Optimization Compiler for Improved Performance in DNN Accelerator Design
preliminary_rationale: Potentially strong co-design/compiler paper, but may be more accelerator-compiler specific than EDA-flow oriented.

title: Compiler Autotuning through Multiple-phase Learning
preliminary_rationale: Relevant for toolchain optimization, though likely closer to compiler autotuning than EDA/co-design proper.

title: NNSmith: Generating Diverse and Valid Test Cases for Deep Learning Compilers
preliminary_rationale: Interesting for validation/toolchain quality, but probably borderline with respect to EDA-specific inclusion.

title: One-shot tuner for deep learning compilers
preliminary_rationale: Potentially useful for toolchain/autotuning discussion, but probably borderline unless you keep compiler-centric studies.

title: C2HLSC: Can LLMs Bridge the Software-to-Hardware Design Gap?
preliminary_rationale: Potentially very relevant to LLM-enabled HW/SW translation, but venue/workshop type should be checked against your IC/EC.

title: DAVE: Deriving automatically verilog from english
preliminary_rationale: Conceptually relevant to natural-language-to-RTL generation, but likely needs venue and peer-review checking.

title: TVM: An automated end-to-end optimizing compiler for deep learning
preliminary_rationale: Important background infrastructure for several co-design papers, but too generic to be safely included as a primary study under your IC/EC.

title: High-Level Synthesis Performance Prediction using GNNs: Benchmarking, Modeling, and Advancing
preliminary_rationale: Highly relevant because it supports early HLS timing/resource estimation from graph representations, reducing costly downstream iterations in the design flow.

title: Machine Learning Based Framework for Fast Resource Estimation of RTL Designs Targeting FPGAs
preliminary_rationale: Highly relevant because it uses ML for early RTL resource estimation and repeated design iteration support, matching the review focus on lifecycle-aware support in EDA/HW-SW design flows.

title: Leveraging Machine Learning for Gate-level Timing Estimation Using Current Source Models and Effective Capacitance
preliminary_rationale: Relevant because it targets timing estimation in the design flow and contributes to the broader family of early QoR/timing prediction approaches.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on Electronic Design Automation and Graph Neural Networks: Theory and Applications
preliminary_rationale: Very useful as a secondary study to structure the graph-based EDA cluster and contextualize multiple prediction/optimization papers.
